plugin.name: Mushkin_Audit
plugin.author: Mushkin Project
plugin.purpose: Behavioral baseline capture for Mushkin vs MUSHclient comparison
plugin.description: Mushkin Audit -- Behavioral baseline capture plugin. Type 'audit' to run all tests and capture API behavior to JSON.

muclient.script:
-- ============================================================ -- JSON Encoder -- Self-contained, no external dependencies. -- Works in Lua 5.1 (MUSHclient) and LuaJIT/5.1-compat (Mushkin). -- Keys are sorted at ALL nesting levels for stable diffs. -- ============================================================
local function is_array(t) -- A table is an array if all keys are consecutive integers starting at 1. local count = 0 for _ in pairs(t) do count = count + 1 end if count == 0 then -- Empty table: treat as array (produces []) return true end for i = 1, count do if t[i] == nil then return false end end return true end
local function json_encode_inner(value, indent) local vtype = type(value)
if value == nil then return "null" elseif vtype == "boolean" then return value and "true" or "false" elseif vtype == "number" then if value ~= value then -- NaN return '"NaN"' elseif value == math.huge then return '"Infinity"' elseif value == -math.huge then return '"-Infinity"' else -- Use integer representation when value is a whole number if value == math.floor(value) and math.abs(value) < 1e15 then return string.format("%d", value) else return string.format("%.17g", value) end end elseif vtype == "string" then -- Escape special characters local s = value s = s:gsub('\\', '\\\\') s = s:gsub('"', '\\"') s = s:gsub('\n', '\\n') s = s:gsub('\r', '\\r') s = s:gsub('\t', '\\t') -- Escape remaining control characters (U+0000–U+001F) s = s:gsub('[\0-\31]', function(c) return string.format('\\u%04x', c:byte()) end) return '"' .. s .. '"' elseif vtype == "table" then local inner_indent = indent .. " "
if is_array(value) then -- Array encoding if #value == 0 then return "[]" end local parts = {} for i = 1, #value do parts[i] = inner_indent .. json_encode_inner(value[i], inner_indent) end return "[\n" .. table.concat(parts, ",\n") .. "\n" .. indent .. "]" else -- Object encoding — collect and sort keys local keys = {} for k in pairs(value) do keys[#keys + 1] = k end -- Sort: numbers first (numerically), then strings (lexicographically) table.sort(keys, function(a, b) local ta, tb = type(a), type(b) if ta == tb then return a < b end -- numbers before strings return ta == "number" end)
if #keys == 0 then return "{}" end
local parts = {} for _, k in ipairs(keys) do -- Use direct lookup — NOT `val[k] or val[tonumber(k)]` -- which would incorrectly skip false values. local v = value[k] local key_str = json_encode_inner(tostring(k), inner_indent) parts[#parts + 1] = inner_indent .. key_str .. ": " .. json_encode_inner(v, inner_indent) end return "{\n" .. table.concat(parts, ",\n") .. "\n" .. indent .. "}" end else -- Functions, userdata, threads: represent as a quoted type string return '"<' .. vtype .. '>"' end end
function json_encode(value) return json_encode_inner(value, "") end
-- ============================================================ -- Test Suite -- ============================================================
local pass_count = 0 local fail_count = 0
local function check(label, got, expected) if got == expected then pass_count = pass_count + 1 ColourNote("lime", "black", " PASS " .. label) else fail_count = fail_count + 1 ColourNote("red", "black", " FAIL " .. label) ColourNote("red", "black", " got: " .. tostring(got)) ColourNote("red", "black", " expected: " .. tostring(expected)) end end
function test_json() pass_count = 0 fail_count = 0
ColourNote("white", "black", "--- json_encode test suite ---")
-- Empty table (array) check("empty table", json_encode({}), "[]")
-- Simple array check("simple array", json_encode({1, 2, 3}), "[\n 1,\n 2,\n 3\n]")
-- Boolean true check("boolean true", json_encode(true), "true")
-- Boolean false check("boolean false", json_encode(false), "false")
-- nil check("nil", json_encode(nil), "null")
-- Integer number check("integer", json_encode(42), "42")
-- Float number check("float", json_encode(3.14), string.format("%.17g", 3.14))
-- NaN check("NaN", json_encode(0/0), '"NaN"')
-- Positive infinity check("Infinity", json_encode(math.huge), '"Infinity"')
-- Negative infinity check("-Infinity", json_encode(-math.huge), '"-Infinity"')
-- Empty string check("empty string", json_encode(""), '""')
-- String with special chars check("string special chars", json_encode('say "hello"\nworld\t!'), '"say \\"hello\\"\\nworld\\t!"')
-- String with backslash check("string backslash", json_encode("a\\b"), '"a\\\\b"')
-- Unicode passthrough (Lua 5.1 strings are byte strings) -- Use decimal escapes (\195\169 = UTF-8 for é) since \xNN is Lua 5.2+ local utf8_e_acute = "\195\169" check("unicode passthrough", json_encode(utf8_e_acute), '"' .. utf8_e_acute .. '"')
-- Simple object with sorted keys check("simple object sorted keys", json_encode({b = 2, a = 1}), '{\n "a": 1,\n "b": 2\n}')
-- Object with false value (must not be skipped) check("object false value", json_encode({flag = false}), '{\n "flag": false\n}')
-- Object with nil value (nil keys are invisible in Lua tables, so this -- tests that a key whose value is false isn't confused with nil) local t = {present = false, missing = nil} check("false vs nil in object", json_encode(t), '{\n "present": false\n}')
-- Nested tables check("nested object", json_encode({outer = {inner = 1}}), '{\n "outer": {\n "inner": 1\n }\n}')
-- Mixed nesting: array of objects check("array of objects", json_encode({{a=1},{a=2}}), '[\n {\n "a": 1\n },\n {\n "a": 2\n }\n]')
-- Mixed integer/string keys — integer keys sort before string keys local mixed = {} mixed[1] = "one" mixed["x"] = "ex" check("mixed keys order", json_encode(mixed), '{\n "1": "one",\n "x": "ex"\n}')
-- Summary local total = pass_count + fail_count ColourNote("white", "black", string.format( "--- %d/%d passed, %d failed ---", pass_count, total, fail_count)) end
-- ============================================================ -- Entry Point -- ============================================================
function run_audit() ColourNote("white", "black", "Mushkin Audit plugin starting...") test_json() ColourNote("white", "black", "Audit plugin ready. Type 'audit' to run.") end
-- Print a startup notice when the plugin loads ColourNote("white", "black", "Mushkin_Audit loaded. Type 'audit' to run.")
